goal: cheap llama.cpp parity: one small GGUF representative per llama.cpp model implementation where certification is meaningful

support_priority.policy: Use P0/P1 as the active support queue. Keep the full llama.cpp inventory classified, but do not block Skippy parity on P2 long-tail, non-causal auxiliary, or implementation-base rows unless a user-visible demand signal appears.
sources: Hugging Face GGUF text-generation search sorted by downloads
Mesh deployment demand for Qwen3.6, DeepSeek V3, Qwen Coder, MiniMax, Falcon-H1, and staged package serving
llama.cpp architecture inventory from the pinned upstream checkout

p0.meaning: must support: very popular GGUF ecosystems, current Mesh deployment targets, or strategic staged/package/multimodal families

p1.meaning: should support: common, historically important, enterprise/vendor, coding, or recurrent families with credible GGUF usage

p2.meaning: best effort: long-tail or niche families. Keep them classified and opportunistically certify when a useful artifact or user demand appears.

candidates.notes: Metal cheap lane passed text split, exact state, and ResidentKv borrowed-hit cache smoke

package-only certified from unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL; skippy-model-package write-package produced a 40-layer package under /Volumes/External/tmp/skippy-family-packages/qwen36-a3b-ud-q4-k-xl and validate-package passed with all 733 tensors accounted for, 43 artifacts checked, no missing/duplicate owned tensors, and matching checksums; runtime cache policy remains KvRecurrent/sticky recurrent ownership
